text:
I'll search for all items simultaneously to check their existence.
Both papers are clearly and thoroughly confirmed by web search results. Here is the verdict:

**ITEM 0 — "The Claude 3 Model Family: Opus, Sonnet, Haiku" (Anthropic, 2024)**


The paper introduces Claude 3, a new family of large multimodal models — Claude 3 Opus, Claude 3 Sonnet, and Claude 3 Haiku.
 
It is hosted on Anthropic's official website
, and 
was developed by Anthropic and announced in March 2024.
 The cited URL resolves directly to the original model card PDF, and the paper is indexed on Semantic Scholar and Papers With Code.

**ITEM 1 — "Protein complex prediction with AlphaFold-Multimer" (Evans et al., bioRxiv, 2022)**


The paper's authors include Richard Evans, Michael O'Neill, Alexander Pritzel, and others, and it addresses the prediction of multi-chain protein complexes.
 
The updated version was posted on March 10, 2022
, consistent with the cited year. It is hosted on bioRxiv (DOI: 10.1101/2021.10.04.463034) and indexed on Semantic Scholar, ResearchGate, and Europe PMC. The original preprint was submitted in 2021, making year "2022" a reference to the revised v2, which is a normal citation variation.
